text:
llm-wiki is an LLM-compiled knowledge base where the agent is both compiler and query engine. The entry model has two layers: immutable `raw/` source documents and synthesized `wiki/` articles. Each wiki article covers exactly one bounded concept (1-3 pages), carries YAML frontmatter with `title`, `category`, `confidence`, `volatility`, `verified`, `sources`, and `summary`, and lives at `wiki/concepts|topics|references/slug.md` with no date in the filename (it's a living document). Cross-references use a dual-link format simultaneously serving both Obsidian (`[[slug|Name]]`) and LLM navigation (`[Name](path.md)`), and are strictly bidirectional — lint enforces it. Every directory has a `_index.md` (a derived cache of titles, summaries, and tags rebuilt from frontmatter when stale), and retrieval uses a 3-hop strategy: read master index → read category index → read matched articles → follow See Also links. The most powerful discipline is "Rule vs Fix": when capturing session knowledge, only the generalizable Rule is written to the wiki, never the specific Fix. [...] 2. **Rule vs Fix distillation in capture.** Every piece of session knowledge gets tested: is this a Fix (what was done this time, discard) or a Rule (the generalizable principle, write to wiki)? Only Rules enter the wiki.
 [...] 1. Distill transcript → Rules (keep our **Rule-vs-Fix** discipline — only Rules persist). [...] ## `capture` (session end) — *grow* the wiki
1. Distill transcript → Rules (keep our **Rule-vs-Fix** discipline — only Rules persist).
2. **Wiki-target** each Rule against `wiki/_index.md`: **create** a new guide, **enrich** an existing one (Edit/append, never rewrite — preserves prior nuance), or fold in as a reference.
3. Add **bidirectional** See-Also links; bump `verified`/`updated`; rebuild `_index.md`; re-index into `index.db`. (Capture can update aggressively — no human gate, since the session is authoritative; pitfall #9.)
